title: V2.5 stabilization and release hardening

sources.reason: Canonical repository governance terminology and architecture boundaries.
Check catalog and status-generation verification references.
English repository overview that describes V2.5 and advanced docs.
Japanese repository overview that must stay aligned with the English README.
Long-term roadmap that positions V2.5 relative to V3.
Reference plan for the implemented V2.5 architecture.
Authoritative status-field names and renderer shape for reviewer-facing examples.

riskAssessment.reason: The change is documentation-only, but multiple reader-facing files must stay consistent with the real status renderer.

executionDecision.reason: Scope, sources, acceptance, and verification are explicit for a docs-only stabilization pass.

preReviewWarnings: Check that all examples match the real generated Cockpit Status headings and recommendation values.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The repository documents V2.5 stabilization and release hardening without expanding into V3 or new schema fields.
Reviewer-facing Cockpit Status examples explain clean ready, ready with medium residual risk, missing verification, unknowns remaining, and intent unresolved cases.
The English and Japanese overview docs stay aligned on the V2.5 release-readiness message.
The generated Cockpit Status file is refreshed through the documented Make target rather than hand-edited.
guidelines: Keep V2.5 limited to stabilization and release hardening; do not introduce V3 Repository Intelligence or schema expansion.
Keep the English and Japanese documentation semantically aligned.
Ensure reviewer-facing examples match the real Cockpit Status renderer.

intent.problem: V2.5 is implemented, but the repository does not yet have a focused stabilization pass that shows reviewers how to read Cockpit Status or how the current recommendation model behaves on common Work Item shapes.
constraints: Do not add V3 Repository Intelligence work.
Do not expand the schema or make Cockpit longer.
Keep the change documentation-first and reviewer-facing.
Keep English and Japanese guidance aligned.
intent.rationale: Documenting the release-hardening behavior, examples, and status interpretation should improve trust in the existing model without increasing product complexity.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
